description: Increases world height to 1024 (-512 to 512) and replaces Bedrock with Reinforced Deepslate.
body:
![A house underneath the minecraft world, with text saying "1024" shown above.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a902abef22bda5a46366a262612b14d4412baa22.png)
This datapack allows you to build with even more room than ever before! Build height in the overworld is extended from the vanilla 384 (-64 to 320) to 1024 (-512 to 512). Not only this, but it also replaces bedrock with reinforced deepslate, allowing you to build underground!


<details>
<summary>SOME PACKS I RECOMMEND TO GO WITH THIS:</summary>
  
[Pickaxe Effective Reinforced Deepslate [PurpurPack]](https://modrinth.com/datapack/purpurpacks-pickaxe-effective-reinforced-deepslate) - Makes it a tad less miserable to get beneath the deepslate

[Silk Touch Reinforced Deepslate [PurpurPack]](https://modrinth.com/datapack/purpurpacks-silk-touch-reinforced-deepslate) - Lets you patch holes in the deepslate after you break them

</details>



Questions I know you'll ask:

<details>
<summary>Why no Nether and End?</summary>
man idk. i'll probably add them in another update?
</details>
<details>
<summary>Why not more blocks? e.g. 2048?</summary>
i would consider 2048, but I made this almost exclusively for a server i'm hosting, and said server is REALLY slow, more blocks = more stress, more stress = slower server :(
</details>
<details>
<summary>Why make it go under bedrock?</summary>
to be honest, i think being able to build under bedrock provides a lot of really fun build opportunities!! like, wanna make skyblock? boom. parkour with an additional amount of fear to it? boom. the opportunities are endless.... 
</details>
<details>
<summary>Why replace bedrock with reinforced deepslate?</summary>
i mean... you're meant to go down there for building reasons now, why would i keep it as impossible to break? i chose reinforced deepslate since i felt it was a good match, really hard block and still deepslate related. i was originally gonna try obsidian, but that felt really boring. 
</details>
